page.title: atopic eczema — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for atopic eczema: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for atopic eczema: 124 alterations and 294 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Serum Vitamin D Level and efficacy of vitamin D supplementation in children with atopic dermatitis
Utilization of light-emitting diodes for skin therapy: Systematic review and meta-analysis.
A meta-analysis suggests a modest role for probiotics in pediatric atopic dermatitis.
Evening primrose oil has therapeutic value in the treatment of atopic eczema.
Lactic acid bacteria as probiotics reduce the risk for infantile eczema.
Selenium levels were associated with several skin diseases and the disease severity.
Supplementation with around 1600 IU/daily of 25-hydroxyvitamin D results in a clinically meaningful atopic dermatitis severity reduction.

The favorable effect of probiotic use in the prevention of atopic dermatitis is similar regardless of the time of probiotic use (pregnancy or early life) or the subject(s) receiving probiotics (mother, child, or both).
The mixed strain of Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium can effectively reduce the incidence of eczema in infants under three years old.
The use of probiotic supplements during pregnancy or during infancy creates a statistically significant decline in the incidence of eczema.
Maternal intake of n-6 polyunsaturated fatty acids, especially linoleic acid, during pregnancy may increase the risk of childhood eczema.
Supplementation of Lactobacillus sakei in children with eczema-dermatitis syndrome is associated with a substantial clinical improvement
Urine phthalate levels were associated with skin barrier dysfunction and atopic sensitization in children
"Fucoidan suppresses IgE production in peripheral blood mononuclear cells from patients with atopic dermatitis."
165 children with atopic dermatitis were studied, a higher number of sensitized food allergens was associated with negative effects on their growth and nutritional status.
20% glycerin is as effective than urea and sodium with less side effects.
: Parents who refuse vaccinations reported less asthma and allergies in their unvaccinated children.
A 1% colloidal oatmeal OTC cream is clinically effective for the management of mild to moderate atopic dermatitis.
A vegetarian diet improves symptoms of atopic dermatitis in adults.
Acupuncture results in a significant reduction in type I hypersensitivity itch in patients with atopic eczema.
Additive effect of Lactobacillus acidophilus L-92 on children with atopic dermatitis concomitant with food allergy.
Advanced effect of Moringa oleifera bioconversion by Rhizopus oligosporus on the treatment of atopic dermatitis.
Air pollution might be an important trigger for atopic dermatitis.
Allergic diseases are less frequent in children with a history of measles.
An over 50% improvement was reported in overall impression and in their skin conditions by all the patients in this study.
Anti-inflammatory properties of raw honey and its clinical applications in daily practice.
Approximately 1 in every 3 patients with atopic eczema are positive for anti-gliadin and/or anti-milk antibodies, indicating they may play a causitive role in the disease.
Association of antioxidants and vitamin D level with inflammation in children with atopic dermatitis.
Beastfeeding has a preventive effect against asthma, ear infection and allergic diseases.
Black currant seed oil supplementation of mothers enhances IFN-γ and suppresses IL-4 production in breast milk.
Blackcurrant seed oil is well tolerated and transiently reduces the prevalence of atopic dermatitis in newborns.
Borage oil is effective in treating atopic dermatitis.
Changing of fecal flora and clinical effect of L. salivarius LS01 in adults with atopic dermatitis.
Childhood chickenpox is associated with decreased atopic disorders, IgE allergic sensitization, and leukocyte subsets.
Children with atopic dermatitis show clinical improvement after Lactobacillus exposure.
Cholesterol-lowering drugs may contribute to skin disorders.
Depressed levels of glutathione peroxidase are observed in patients with various skin disorders which are restored by selenium and vitamin E treatment.
Direct infant UV light exposure is associated with eczema and immune development.
Docosahexaenoic acid effect on prenatal exposure to arsenic and atopic dermatitis in Mexican preschoolers.
Drinking deep-sea water restores mineral imbalance in atopic eczema/dermatitis syndrome.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

natural_products.name: Selenium
natural_products.key_findings: Meta Analysis, Review: Selenium levels were associated with several skin diseases and the disease severity.; Human Study: Depressed levels of glutathione peroxidase are observed in patients with various skin disorders which are restored by selenium and vitamin E treatm

natural_products.name: Moringa
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Advanced effect of Moringa oleifera bioconversion by Rhizopus oligosporus on the treatment of atopic dermatitis.

natural_products.name: Fermented Foods and Beverages
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Advanced effect of Moringa oleifera bioconversion by Rhizopus oligosporus on the treatment of atopic dermatitis.

natural_products.name: Homeopathic Medicine: All
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: An over 50% improvement was reported in overall impression and in their skin conditions by all the patients in this study.

natural_products.name: Fucoidan
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: "Fucoidan suppresses IgE production in peripheral blood mononuclear cells from patients with atopic dermatitis."

natural_products.name: Evening Primrose Oil
natural_products.key_findings: Meta Analysis: Evening primrose oil has therapeutic value in the treatment of atopic eczema.

natural_products.name: Lactobacillus probiotics
natural_products.key_findings: Meta Analysis: Lactic acid bacteria as probiotics reduce the risk for infantile eczema.

natural_products.name: Bifidobacterium Longum
natural_products.key_findings: Meta Analysis: The mixed strain of Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium can effectively reduce the incidence of eczema in infants under three years old.

natural_products.name: Black Currant
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Black currant seed oil supplementation of mothers enhances IFN-γ and suppresses IL-4 production in breast milk.; Human Study: Blackcurrant seed oil is well tolerated and transiently reduces the prevalence of atopic dermatitis in newborns.

natural_products.name: Lactobacillus salivarius
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Changing of fecal flora and clinical effect of L. salivarius LS01 in adults with atopic dermatitis.

natural_products.name: Lactobacillus sakei
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Supplementation of Lactobacillus sakei in children with eczema-dermatitis syndrome is associated with a substantial clinical improvement

natural_products.name: Oats
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: A 1% colloidal oatmeal OTC cream is clinically effective for the management of mild to moderate atopic dermatitis.

natural_products.name: Honey
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Anti-inflammatory properties of raw honey and its clinical applications in daily practice.

natural_products.name: Lactobacillus fermentum
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Children with atopic dermatitis show clinical improvement after Lactobacillus exposure.

natural_products.name: Water: Deep Sea
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Drinking deep-sea water restores mineral imbalance in atopic eczema/dermatitis syndrome.

natural_products.name: Streptococcus thermophilus
natural_products.key_findings: Meta Analysis: Lactic acid bacteria as probiotics reduce the risk for infantile eczema.

natural_products.name: Peanut
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: 165 children with atopic dermatitis were studied, a higher number of sensitized food allergens was associated with negative effects on their growth

natural_products.name: Borage Oil
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Borage oil is effective in treating atopic dermatitis.

natural_products.name: Lactobacillus paracasei
natural_products.key_findings: Human Study: Children with atopic dermatitis show clinical improvement after Lactobacillus exposure.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: 60-70% of childhood-onset AD achieves spontaneous remission by adolescence; adult-onset AD is more persistent; moderate-severe adult AD: <15% spontaneous clearance at 5 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Dupilumab (anti-IL-4Rα): 51% IGA 0/1 (clear/almost clear) at 16 weeks in SOLO trials; tralokinumab (anti-IL-13): 38% clear/almost clear; baricitinib (JAK1/2i): 16% EASI-100 (complete clearance) at 16 weeks; upadacitinib (JAK1i): 28-40% EASI-100; near-complete clearance now achievable for most patients
remission.gap_size: Large — most patients managed with topical steroids for decades without biologic assessment; biologics available since 2017 but reach only a fraction of eligible patients
remission.barrier_detail: Dupilumab approved 2017 but still not first-line in most health systems due to cost ($36,000/year); step therapy requirements force patients through years of topical steroids and immunosuppressants first; racial disparities in specialist access and biologic prescribing
remission.last_soc_change: 2022-2023 — JAK inhibitors (upadacitinib, baricitinib) and tralokinumab added; lebrikizumab approved 2023; dupilumab since 2017; prior standard (topical steroids, tacrolimus, cyclosporine) essentially unchanged since 1980s-2000s
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term topical corticosteroid on sensitive skin areas (periocular, genital) without switching to non-steroidal alternatives; wet wrap therapy without concurrent assessment for systemic options; systemic corticosteroid use beyond acute flare control (rebound flare risk on tapering; still over-relied upon)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Microbiome restoration (S. epidermidis transplant — Phase 2 data for AD); proactive maintenance therapy (applying tacrolimus 2x/week to previously affected areas — reduces flare frequency 50%); bleach baths (reduce S. aureus colonisation, reduces flare severity); vitamin D supplementation in deficient patients
remission.frontier_signal: IL-31 inhibitor (nemolizumab — FDA approved 2024 for AD and prurigo nodularis); CRTH2/DP2 antagonist (fevipiprant); OX40 pathway inhibitors (rocatinlimab Phase 3); IL-33 inhibitors (itepekimab for asthma expanding to AD); oral PDE4 inhibitors (difamilast — approved Japan; pending US)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
